riskTone.note: Gold up on softer dollar & trimmed Fed hike bets post-PCE, but hawkish Fed & geopolitical risks (Strait of Hormuz) cap gains.

overnight: Gold gained as recent US PCE inflation data weighed on the dollar, leading markets to slightly trim Fed rate-hike bets. Spot gold traded higher, though still on track for a fourth consecutive weekly decline. DXY dipped but held near recent highs. Crude prices fell as Strait of Hormuz supply concerns eased, but new attack reports add caution.
sectorDrivers: Gold supported by slightly lower real yields (10Y TIPS eased to 2.17%) & a weaker dollar (DXY down 0.22%) post-PCE data. Hawkish Fed outlook (3 hikes expected, 61-63% Sep hike chance) remains a key headwind. Geopolitical risks (ME, Ukraine) provide haven demand. Strong central bank (45% plan to add) & physical demand offers structural support.

keyEvents.note: Reflects US economic health, influencing dollar & Fed outlook.
Key for inflation expectations & consumer spending, impacting Fed path.
Fed official comments can signal policy shifts and influence rate expectations.

headlines.headline: PRECIOUS-Gold heads for fourth weekly drop as traders reassess Fed rate outlook
headlines.note: Captures gold's struggle despite today's bounce, driven by persistent Fed hawkishness.

headlines.headline: Gold Rebounds Above $4060 as PCE Eases Rate-Hike Fears; Silver Advances 1.3%

headlines.headline: Record 45% of central banks plan to increase gold holdings, WGC survey finds
headlines.note: Highlights strong structural demand, providing a floor despite short-term volatility.
